Virtual Staging AI is a company based in Wilmington (United States) founded in 2023 by Mikhail Andreev and Michael Bonacina was acquired by Zillow Group in October 2024.. Virtual Staging AI offers products and services including Virtual Staging App, Multi-View Staging, and Furniture Removal. Virtual Staging AI operates in a competitive market with competitors including Matterport, Backbone, Trezi, iStaging and Cylindo, among others.

steps.result:
[{"url": "https://qz.com/zillow-group-inc-z-reports-earnings-1851760899", "title": "Zillow Group Inc. (Z) reports earnings", "content": "The filing reports total revenue of $2.2 billion, a 15% increase from $1.9 billion in 2023. This growth was driven by increases in Residential and Mortgages revenue, as well as Rentals revenue.

#### Related Content

Google joins the Dow — and the index is set to close above 52,000 for the first time

Alphabet joins the Dow — but AI doubts are weighing on its stock", "score": 0.83932644, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://eliteagent.com/forget-about-making-zillions-zillow-posted-a-net-loss-for-2023", "title": "Forget about making zillions, Zillow posted a net loss for 2023", "content": "According to the figures, fourth quarter revenue was up 9 per cent year-on-year to US$474 million and above the company’s outlook range by $31 million.

But that didn’t stop full-year revenue dropping 1 per cent to $1.9 billion.

Residential revenue climbed 3 per cent year-on-year in Q4 to US$349 million, while rentals revenue jumped 37 per cent, year-on-year, to $93 million, primarily driven by multifamily revenue rising 52 per cent in the last three months of 2023.", "score": 0.8095324, "raw_content": null}, {"url": "https://finance.yahoo.com/news/zillow-group-inc-zg-reports-213322915.html", "title": "Zillow Group Inc (ZG) Reports 3% Revenue Growth in Q3 2023", "content": "Rentals revenue of $99 million increased 34% year over year, driven primarily by the companys multifamily revenue, which grew 42% year over year in Q3 2023. steps.args:
{'prompt': 'From this Zillow Group 10-K for fiscal year 2024, please extract:
1. The revenue breakdown by category (Residential, Rentals, Mortgages, Other) for fiscal years 2024, 2023, and 2022, with exact dollar amounts in millions.
2. Any details about business acquisitions completed in 2023 and 2024 (e.g., Follow Up Boss, Aryeo, Spruce, Virtual Staging AI, etc.), including purchase prices, dates, and stated strategic rationale.
3. Any description of the company's strategy (e.g., "housing super app", enhanced markets, growth pillars) related to these acquisitions.

Document: {{zillow_10k_2024}}'}
steps.result:
# Zillow Group FY2024 10-K Extraction

## 1. Revenue Breakdown by Category (in millions)

| Category | FY 2024 | FY 2023 | FY 2022 |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Residential** | $1,594 | $1,452 | $1,522 |
| **Mortgages** | $145 | $96 | $119 |
| *For Sale subtotal (Residential + Mortgages)* | *$1,739* | *$1,548* | *$1,641* |
| **Rentals** | $453 | $357 | $274 |
| **Other** | $44 | $40 | $43 |
| **Total revenue** | **$2,236** | **$1,945** | **$1,958** |

Notable YoY commentary (2024 vs. 2023): Residential +$142M (+10%); Mortgages +$49M (+51%, driven by loan origination volume doubling to $3.1B); Rentals +$96M (+27%); Total revenue +15%. Residential was 71% of total revenue in 2024.

## 2. Business Acquisitions (2023–2024)

**Follow Up Boss (Enchant, LLC)** — Acquired **December 8, 2023**
- Consideration: **$399 million in cash, net of cash acquired** ($403M gross cash), plus **contingent consideration of up to $100 million** payable over a three-year period upon achievement of certain performance metrics (initial fair value of contingent consideration: $81M; total purchase price allocated: $484M)
- Purchase price allocation: Goodwill $402M (tax deductible); intangibles $86M (developed technology $50M / 4-yr life; customer relationships $34M / 7-yr; trade names/trademarks $2M / 7-yr); deferred revenue $(7)M
- First earn-out period passed as of Dec 31, 2024, resulting in an expected **$33 million payment in February 2025**
- Stated rationale: "The acquisition is consistent with our strategy to invest in a more integrated software experience for our customers." Follow Up Boss is a CRM system giving real estate agents, teams, and brokerages "a central hub to organize and engage customers, close deals and build out their teams."

**Aryeo, Inc.** — Acquired **July 31, 2023**
- Consideration: **~$15 million cash (net of cash acquired) + 380,259 shares of Class C capital stock valued at $20 million = $35 million total** (net of cash acquired; $38M gross purchase price)
- A software company serving **real estate photographers**
- Allocation: Goodwill $26M (not tax deductible); intangibles $11M (customer relationships $5M / 5-yr; purchased content $4M / 3-yr; developed technology $2M / 3-yr)

**Spruce (substantially all assets/liabilities of Spruce Holdings, Inc.)** — Acquired **September 11, 2023**
- Consideration: **~$19 million cash, net of cash acquired** ($24M gross purchase price)
- A **tech-enabled title and escrow platform**
- Allocation: Goodwill $16M (tax deductible); developed technology intangible $2M / 3-yr life

**Virtual Staging AI Inc.** — Acquired **October 2024**
- Mentioned in the risk factors list of acquisitions; **no purchase price or detailed terms disclosed** in this 10-K (2024 cash flow shows $7M "cash paid for acquisitions, net," which may relate to this deal). (VRX Media Group LLC, acquired December 2022, is also referenced.)

The company noted aggregate acquisition-related costs for Follow Up Boss, Aryeo, and Spruce were immaterial, and pro forma results were not presented because the acquisitions were not material to the consolidated financial statements.

## 3. Strategy Related to These Acquisitions

- **"Housing super app":** Zillow describes helping "renters, buyers, sellers, and real estate professionals across all their residential real estate needs through our 'housing super app' which serves as an ecosystem of connected solutions for the tasks and services related to moving."

- **Three growth/investment areas (pillars):**
  1. **For Sale** — Centered on **Enhanced Markets**, defined as "geographies where Zillow is investing to provide an integrated home-buying experience, enabling consumers to digitally and seamlessly move through the process—from touring to closing—with support from Premier Agent partners and Zillow Home Loans loan officers." As of December 31, 2024, Zillow was active in **43 Enhanced Markets representing 21% of total connections**. Zillow Showcase went nationwide in 2024 (~1.7% of new for-sale listings).
  2. **Industry Software** — This is where the acquisitions fit strategically: "As examples, we acquired **Aryeo and Follow Up Boss**, which provide real estate professionals tools to manage leads, create listings, and effectuate transactions." The stated aim is "digital solutions to empower real estate professionals to drive a better consumer experience for movers." Notably, as of December 31, 2024, **more than 80% of Enhanced Market connections are managed through Follow Up Boss**, tying the acquisition directly to the Enhanced Markets strategy.
  3. **Rentals Marketplace** — Building a nationwide rentals marketplace (including a 2024 Realtor.com listings partnership and, per the subsequent event note, a February 2025 Redfin partnership with a $100M upfront payment).

- Spruce (title and escrow) supports the integrated transaction experience — the company lists it among the "comprehensive suite of marketing software and technology solutions for the real estate industry, including ShowingTime+, Follow Up Boss and Spruce" that deliver "seamless, integrated" transaction experiences from touring to closing.

steps.args:
{'final_result': '## Zillow's Acquisition Strategy (2023–2024) and Its Alignment with the Evolving Revenue Mix

### 1. Acquisitions over the past two years (Dec 2022 – Dec 2024)

Zillow has pursued a series of relatively small, targeted "tuck-in" acquisitions — a deliberate contrast to its former capital-intensive iBuying strategy (Zillow Offers, wound down in 2021). All of the deals target **agent-facing software and transaction-workflow technology** rather than balance-sheet-heavy businesses:

| Acquisition | Date | Consideration | What it does |
|---|---|---|---|
| **VRX Media Group** | Dec 2022 | Not material/disclosed | Real estate media/photography services |
| **Aryeo, Inc.** | Jul 31, 2023 | ~$35M net ($15M cash + 380,259 Class C shares valued at $20M) | Listing media/content software for real estate photographers |
| **Spruce** (assets of Spruce Holdings) | Sep 11, 2023 | ~$19M cash, net | Tech-enabled title and escrow platform |
| **Follow Up Boss** (Enchant, LLC) | Dec 8, 2023 | $399M cash, net + up to $100M contingent earnout (initial fair value $81M; first $33M earnout earned and payable Feb 2025) | Leading CRM for real estate agents, teams and brokerages |
| **Virtual Staging AI (VSAI)** | Oct 2024 | Small/undisclosed (2024 cash flow shows only $7M paid for acquisitions, net) | AI-powered virtual staging of listing photos |

**The strategic thread:** Every deal supports Zillow's "housing super app" vision and, in particular, its **"Industry Software"** growth pillar and **Enhanced Markets** strategy — building an integrated, digital home-buying/selling experience from touring to closing. Per the 10-K, Zillow explicitly states the Follow Up Boss acquisition "is consistent with our strategy to invest in a more integrated software experience for our customers," and that Aryeo and Follow Up Boss "provide real estate professionals tools to manage leads, create listings, and effectuate transactions." Critically, by December 31, 2024, **more than 80% of connections in Zillow's 43 Enhanced Markets were being managed through Follow Up Boss**, and its suite of ShowingTime+, Follow Up Boss and Spruce delivers "seamless, integrated" transaction experiences. In short: Zillow is buying the software layer that keeps agents, transactions, and ultimately mortgage/title attach rates inside its ecosystem, plus AI tooling (VSAI) to enrich listings (e.g., Zillow Showcase, which reached ~1.7% of all new U.S. for-sale listings in 2024).

### 2. Evolving revenue mix (FY2022 → FY2024, per the FY2024 10-K)

| Category | 2022 | 2023 | 2024 | Mix 2022 | Mix 2023 | Mix 2024 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Residential | $1,522M | $1,452M | $1,594M | 77.73% | 74.65% | 71.29% |
| Rentals | $274M | $357M | $453M | 13.99% | 18.35% | 20.26% |
| Mortgages | $119M | $96M | $145M | 6.08% | 4.94% | 6.48% |
| Other | $43M | $40M | $44M | 2.20% | 2.06% | 1.97% |
| **Total** | **$1,958M** | **$1,945M** | **$2,236M** | 100% | 100% | 100% |

Key mix shifts through December 2024:
- **Residential's share fell from 77.73% (2022) to 71.29% (2024)** even as it grew 9.78% in dollar terms in 2024 (to $1,594M), aided by contributions from Follow Up Boss, ShowingTime+ and New Construction.
- **Rentals is the fastest-growing diversifier**, up 27% in 2024 to $453M and rising from 13.99% to 20.26% of revenue.
- **Mortgages rebounded sharply**, up 51.04% in 2024 to $145M (6.48% of mix), with purchase loan origination volume roughly doubling to ~$3.1B — a direct payoff of the Enhanced Markets integration.
- Total revenue grew 14.96% in 2024 to $2,236M despite housing transactions at ~30-year lows, and net loss margin improved to ~5% from 8%.

### 3. How the acquisitions align with the revenue mix evolution

- **Deepening, not just diversifying, the core:** The acquisitions are concentrated on the "For Sale" side (CRM, showing management, listing media, title/escrow). They monetize primarily through Residential (Premier Agent, software/Showcase) — helping Residential return to growth (+10% in 2024) even in a weak housing market, and adding recurring, software-like revenue streams (Follow Up Boss added a SaaS CRM business with $86M of identified technology/customer intangibles).
- **Driving mortgage attach:** The Enhanced Markets flywheel — where >80% of connections run through Follow Up Boss — is designed to convert Zillow's audience into Zillow Home Loans originations. The 51% Mortgages revenue growth (and 86% in Q4 2024) is the clearest evidence of this alignment: acquisitions of workflow software keep the transaction inside Zillow's funnel so it can capture mortgage (and eventually title/escrow via Spruce) economics.
- **Capital-light discipline:** The combined outlay (~$460M net cash across all five deals, dominated by Follow Up Boss) is modest relative to Zillow's ~$1.9B cash and investments, reflecting a post-iBuying strategy of small, high-leverage technology tuck-ins rather than transformational M&A.
- **Rentals grew organically, not via M&A:** Notably, the fastest-diversifying segment (Rentals, now 20.26% of revenue) was built through partnerships (e.g., the March 2024 Realtor.com syndication deal, and a $100M Redfin partnership announced February 2025) rather than acquisitions — underscoring that Zillow's M&A dollars over the past two years were deployed specifically to integrate the for-sale transaction and boost per-transaction monetization (agent software + mortgage attach), while the mix nonetheless became more balanced overall.

**Bottom line:** Over 2023–2024 Zillow ran a focused, capital-light acquisition strategy — Aryeo, Spruce, Follow Up Boss, and Virtual Staging AI — aimed at owning the agent software stack and end-to-end transaction workflow ("housing super app"/Enhanced Markets). This aligns tightly with the revenue mix evolution: Residential remains dominant but is becoming a more software-and-services-enriched revenue stream, Mortgages revenue is re-accelerating (+51% in 2024) as the acquired software funnels transactions to Zillow Home Loans, and total revenue diversification has increased, with Residential's share of revenue declining from 77.73% in 2022 to 71.29% in 2024 while Rentals and Mortgages together grew from 20.07% to 26.74% of the mix.

{
    "sources": [
        {
            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1617640/000161764025000016/z-20241231.htm",
            "name": "Zillow Group, Inc. Form 10-K for fiscal year 2024 (filed Feb 11, 2025) - SEC EDGAR"
        },
        {
            "url": "https://www.geekwire.com/2024/zillow-group-acquires-vsai-a-virtual-staging-tool-that-adds-furniture-to-real-estate-listings",
            "name": "GeekWire - Zillow Group acquires VSAI (Virtual Staging AI), October 2024"
        },
        {
            "url": "https://www.geekwire.com/2023/zillow-acquiring-follow-up-boss-for-up-to-500m-in-quest-for-housing-super-app",
            "name": "GeekWire - Zillow acquiring Follow Up Boss for up to $500M"
        },
        {
            "url": "https://www.marketscreener.com/quote/stock/ZILLOW-GROUP-INC-20814107/news/Zillow-Fourth-Quarter-2024-Shareholder-Letter-49025456",
            "name": "Zillow Fourth Quarter 2024 Shareholder Letter (via MarketScreener)"
        },
        {
            "url": "https://www.onlinemarketplaces.com/articles/zillow-q4-2024-and-fy-2024-results-double-digit-revenue-growth-as-net-losses-narrow",
            "name": "Online Marketplaces - Zillow Q4 2024 and FY 2024 Results"
        },
        {
            "url": "https://www.bizjournals.com/boston/news/2023/08/03/zillow-buys-aryeo-real-estate-tech-35m.html",
            "name": "Boston Business Journal - Zillow pays $35M for Aryeo"
        }
    ]
}'}

### 1. Acquisitions over the past two years (Dec 2022 – Dec 2024)

Zillow has pursued a series of relatively small, targeted "tuck-in" acquisitions — a deliberate contrast to its former capital-intensive iBuying strategy (Zillow Offers, wound down in 2021). **The strategic thread:** Every deal supports Zillow's "housing super app" vision and, in particular, its **"Industry Software"** growth pillar and **Enhanced Markets** strategy — building an integrated, digital home-buying/selling experience from touring to closing. Per the 10-K, Zillow explicitly states the Follow Up Boss acquisition "is consistent with our strategy to invest in a more integrated software experience for our customers," and that Aryeo and Follow Up Boss "provide real estate professionals tools to manage leads, create listings, and effectuate transactions." Critically, by December 31, 2024, **more than 80% of connections in Zillow's 43 Enhanced Markets were being managed through Follow Up Boss**, and its suite of ShowingTime+, Follow Up Boss and Spruce delivers "seamless, integrated" transaction experiences. In short: Zillow is buying the software layer that keeps agents, transactions, and ultimately mortgage/title attach rates inside its ecosystem, plus AI tooling (VSAI) to enrich listings (e.g., Zillow Showcase, which reached ~1.7% of all new U.S. for-sale listings in 2024).

- **Mortgages rebounded sharply**, up 51.04% in 2024 to $145M (6.48% of mix), with purchase loan origination volume roughly doubling to ~$3.1B — a direct payoff of the Enhanced Markets integration.
- Total revenue grew 14.96% in 2024 to $2,236M despite housing transactions at ~30-year lows, and net loss margin improved to ~5% from 8%.

- **Deepening, not just diversifying, the core:** The acquisitions are concentrated on the "For Sale" side (CRM, showing management, listing media, title/escrow).
